title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object Reference

statement.markdown: You are given a React memoized component `MemoizedComponent` that uses the `useState` hook to manage its local state. The component also uses the `useMemo` hook to memoize a complex calculation based on its state. However, the component re-renders unnecessarily when the state changes, even if the calculation result remains the same. Your task is to optimize the component to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: A JSON object containing the component's state and props.
statement.notes: Make sure to use memoization correctly to avoid unnecessary re-renders.

constraints.description: The number of times the component's state is updated

examples.explanation: The component should re-render when the state or props change.
The component should not re-render when the state remains the same.

hints.text: Use the `memo` hook to memoize the calculation result.

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object Reference
editorial.intuition: Memoization is used to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.bruteForce: Re-render the component on every state change.
editorial.betterSolution: Use memoization to cache the calculation result.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `memo` hook to memoize the calculation result and avoid unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component re-renders only when the state or props change.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the optimized component.
